Pray, I entreat thee, stifle thy clattering tongue.
​It wouldst be a mercy to the realm if thou shouldst seal those lips, for the incessant tempest of thy breath doth weary my very soul. As the sun seeketh the horizon to grant the world a reprieve of silence, so too shouldst thou grant thy speech a rest. topQuote.author: Charlie
topQuote.text: Hark, thou most insufferable, prattling, babbling, ceaseless, wind-addled, tongue-wagging, ear-assaulting creature that the good Lord in some moment of most grievous miscalculation saw fit to bestow upon this already suffering world a voice — I do beseech thee, nay, I do IMPLORE thee, nay, I do fall upon mine very knees and cry out unto the heavens above and the depths below and every realm that doth exist betwixt the two, that thou wouldst, in some miraculous and long-overdue act of divine mercy toward all those assembled here who have suffered most tremendously beneath the crushing, relentless, unstoppable avalanche of words, syllables, murmurs, utterances, proclamations, declarations, and general sonic catastrophes that have so freely and so mercilessly poured forth from that great and terrible cavern thou dost have the audacity to call a mouth — that thou wouldst, at long last, after what hath felt to mine weary ears like several geological ages of unbroken torment, CEASE. Desist. Halt. Stop. Conclude. Terminate. Bring to a close. Draw the curtain. Extinguish the flame. Still the waters. Quiet the tempest. Silence the bells. For as God is my witness and the stars above are mine testament, there hath not been heard in all the kingdoms of this earth, nor in any tale told by poet or prophet or wandering fool at a tavern fire, a noise so relentlessly, so magnificently, so breathtakingly unnecessary as the sound of thy voice continuing to exist in a world that hath done nothing — nothing, I say — to deserve it, and if thou dost not, in this very moment, close thine lips, seal thine jaw, retire thy tongue to the dark and blessed silence from whence it should never have emerged, I shall be forced to conclude that thou art not merely a fool, but the KING of fools, the EMPEROR of fools, the undisputed and eternal sovereign of all foolishness across every age and every land, seated upon a throne built entirely from the wreckage of every conversation thou hast ever ruined, and wearing upon thy head a crown fashioned from the collective exhaustion of every poor soul who hath ever had the profound misfortune of standing within earshot of thee — SO. SHUT. UP.

topQuote.author: Charlie
topQuote.text: (Me serving Tyler, The Creator food before closing) Tyler: "Thanks again for the food." Me: "No problem have a good night!" Tyler: *goes to leave* Tyler: "That's strange the door is locked?" Me: "Really? Huh, I don't know why." (Me knowing I locked it) Me: "You know Tyler, sense it's just me and you in here, I wanted to tell you I'm in love with you." Tyler: "Really? I think I am too!" *Tyler blushes and turns his head away* Me: "No need to be embarrassed." *I say as I'm running my hands through his hair* Me: "How bout I spend 48 hours making your new magic wand sticky?" Tyler: "I like the sound of that." *Me and Tyler start making rough passionate love on the restaurant floor*

topQuote.author: Charlie
topQuote.text:
"The first flight to Mars would carry a simulated crew consisting of one or more robots of the Tesla-built humanoid Optimus design, with the first human crews following in the second or third landings."
reuters.com/business/aerospace-defense/musk-aiming-send-uncrewed-starship-mars-by-end-2026-2025-05-30
Robots go first. Humans follow once the groundwork is done. Makes sense.
"Five uncrewed Starships would lift off in 2026... touch down on Mars in 2027, carrying an unspecified number of the bipedal Optimus robot."
aerospaceamerica.aiaa.org/aiaa-spacex
2027 is the real starting gun for this whole thing.
"Musk said SpaceX will use Tesla Optimus robots to lay the groundwork and construct the Mars colony. The first human missions are set for 2027."
decrypt.co/323042/elon-musk-spacex-mars-plan-self-sustaining-colony
This confirms your exact theory. Robots build it, humans arrive later.
"SpaceX envisions a self-sustaining Mars colony by 2050. Independent experts say 2060-2080 is more realistic."
optimusk.blog/blog/mars-colonization-humans-vs-robots
